rows.description: Computus Dni Rectoris I L Richards a 2die Novbr AD 1824 ad eundem diem revolution 1825
rows.notes: Top-of-page title is partly faded and written in a cramped hand. I transcribed the Latin-like phrase and date as it appears; some letters (e.g. exact spelling of names and 'revolution') are ambiguous, so confidence is moderate.
Centered section heading. Letters are fairly clear though the first word's spelling (Arrearagia/Arrearagia) is slightly uncertain; nonetheless clearly a section label with no amounts.
Clear centered header introducing the list of fixed money rents. No amounts on this row.

Single number '60' aligned under the pounds column (based on alignment with multi-column rows below such as '1 7 6'). The number is clear but there is some ambiguity whether it might represent shillings in an alternate layout; column alignment supports £60.
Single clear numeral '10' in the pounds column. Handwriting of place name is legible; amount alignment supports pounds rather than shillings.
Clear three-part amount '1 7 6' (pounds, shillings, pence). Description 'Do Capras & Broad Do' reproduced as written; numbers are well-aligned and pass validation.
Amount reads as '9 6 8' clearly in the three narrow columns. The place-name 'Lad Lane late Norris' is legible though late/Norris spelling is slightly uncertain; numbers validate correctly.
Indented/sub-item 'Chaplin...' with amount '6 13 4'. The description has abbreviation/uncertain characters ('15. Midn') but the numeric columns are clear and in-range.
Description appears as 'Pitchford 13' (some uncertainty about the numeral after the place). Amount '1 13 4' is aligned and legible.
Amount appears as '16 13 9' with a trailing small loop/mark after the 9 that resembles a '3q' (three farthings) symbol; interpreted as 0.75. The fraction mark is faint so confidence is moderate.

rows.description: Little Ton Colman late Bucks 1 yr
rows.notes: Description read as 'Little Ton Colman late Bucks' (spacing/compound name slightly uncertain). Amount '1 6 1' is clear and aligned.
Recorded as '17 6' in the leftmost two money columns. The writing of the place name is faint/abbreviated ('Lymath'); pence column is blank. Interpreted as £17 6s (no pence).
A struck-through/cancelled line (handwriting heavily crossed out). No amounts visible on this row. The place-name reading is uncertain due to crossing out.
Clear centered section header. No monetary values on this row.
Amount reads '11 2 9' clearly. Description '1/2 yr' (half year) is legible; alignment confirms columns.
Single '28' visible in the pounds column. Place name 'Long Witherham' is fairly clear; no shillings/pence written.
Single '14' in pounds column. The script after 'Garston Rectory' (the '13' or other numeral) is faint/abbreviated but the amount is distinct.
Section heading clearly written and centered. No amounts present on this row.
Amount '91 2 6' is clear in the three narrow columns. Description contains 'Augmentation 1/3' which is legible.
Amount '102 7 5' is clear. The place name repeats 'Long Witherham Rectory'; a minor uncertainty in the trailing year notation does not affect amounts.

Pounds/shillings/pence read as '171 11 4' with a small halfpenny symbol after the 4 (interpreted as 0.5). The place-name is uncertain (hand reads like 'St Nawsiton' or 'St Newington'); numeric columns are reasonably clear.
Read as 'Do Tenement 3 yr' with amount '7 10 2' clear and well-aligned. High confidence in numbers and 'Do' abbreviation meaning 'Ditto'.
Amount '238 15 10' is clear. The description includes 'Tax deducted' and a numeric note '15s' which is somewhat abbreviated in the script; amount alignment is unambiguous.
Amount '44 9 4' is clear and correctly aligned. Description reads 'Garston Rectory 15 yr' though the year numeral is slightly abbreviated.
Final total line reads '806 17 1/2' — interpreted as £806, 17s, and a halfpenny (0 pence + 0.5). Numeric alignment is clear and consistent with other totals on the page.
